question: Illustrate access specifiers (`public`, `private`, `protected`). Create a base class `Person` with `public` name, `protected` age, and `private` socialSecurityNumber. Derive a `Student` class and try to access these members, explaining which are accessible and why.

test_cases.expected:
--- Access from main function ---
Accessing public member 'name': Charlie Green
Attempting to access protected member 'age': Not accessible directly from main (outside derived class).
Attempting to access private member 'socialSecurityNumber': Not accessible directly from main.

--- Student Info (from Student class) ---
Accessing public member 'name': Charlie Green
Accessing protected member 'age': 21
Student ID: S1003
Attempting to access private member 'socialSecurityNumber': <COMPILER ERROR>

driver: int main() { Student s("Charlie Green", 21, "111-22-3333", "S1003"); std::cout << "--- Access from main function ---" << std::endl; std::cout << "Accessing public member 'name': " << s.name << std::endl; std::cout << "Attempting to access protected member 'age': Not accessible directly from main (outside derived class)." << std::endl; std::cout << "Attempting to access private member 'socialSecurityNumber': Not accessible directly from main." << std::endl; s.displayStudentInfo(); return 0; }
errors: Attempt to access a 'private' member 'socialSecurityNumber' from the derived class 'Student'. Private members are only accessible within the class in which they are declared, not in derived classes.
